title: Extending further
text: extend ggblanket , users can: Use ggblank function Work non-default stat’s Use non-supported aesthetics Use non-supported geoms Modifying part colouring Create custom wrapper functions Visualise spatial data Use different fonts Work extension packages
code: library(dplyr) library(stringr) library(ggplot2) library(ggblanket) library(palmerpenguins) library(patchwork)  penguins <- penguins |>   mutate(sex = str_to_sentence(sex)) |>   tidyr::drop_na(sex)

title: Extending further
text: ggblank function flexible function ggblanket. provides access aesthetics. provides lots flexibility use ggblanket ggplot2 layers extension packages. Remember gg_* function builds x y scales based data, stat, x, y gg_* function - can adjusted using facet_scales, *_include *_limits arguments. plot scales constructed without knowledge subsequent layers. ggblank, must include ggplot2 stat gg_blank function deviates "identity". stat must character, stat functions supported.
code: penguins |>   gg_blank(     x = body_mass_g,      stat = "bin") +   geom_histogram(col = pal_blue, fill = pal_blue, alpha = 0.9)

title: Extending further
text: Users can change default stat. … argument provides access relevant stat_* function well geom_* function. Note, gg_* functions support using stat function - must character (e.g. “summary”).
code: penguins |>   gg_errorbar(     x = body_mass_g,     y = species,     stat = "summary",     fun.data = \(x) mean_se(x, 1.96),     width = 0.25,     x_include = 0   ) +   geom_bar(     stat = "summary",     fun = \(x) mean(x),     alpha = 0.2,     width = 0.75)

title: Extending further
text: aesthetics colouring supported ggblanket, possible access plots made combination gg_blank ggplot2 code. Discrete aesthetic   Continuous aesthetic
code: penguins |>   gg_blank(     x = bill_length_mm,     y = body_mass_g,      col = sex) + #hack for legend placement   geom_point(aes(shape = sex), col = "#2B6999") +    labs(shape = "Sex") #name same as the col_title penguins |>   gg_blank(     x = bill_length_mm,     y = body_mass_g,      col = sex) +    geom_point(aes(shape = sex)) +     labs(shape = "Sex") #name same as the col_title penguins |>   gg_blank(     x = bill_length_mm,     y = body_mass_g) +     geom_point(aes(alpha = bill_depth_mm), col = "#2B6999") +   labs(alpha = "Bill depth mm") penguins |>   gg_blank(     x = bill_length_mm,     y = body_mass_g,      col = species,     col_legend_place = "r", #two legends works better on right     col_title = "\nSpecies") + #add space between legends   geom_point(aes(alpha = bill_depth_mm)) +   labs(alpha = "Bill depth mm")

title: Extending further
text: gblanket support ggplot2 geoms. However, can accessed via gg_blank function.
code: p1 <- faithful |>    gg_blank(x = eruptions,             y = waiting,             x_breaks = scales::breaks_pretty(3),             title = "geom_density_2d") +    geom_density2d(aes(col = after_stat(level)),                  show.legend = FALSE)  p2 <- faithful |>   gg_blank(x = waiting,             y = eruptions,             x_breaks = scales::breaks_pretty(3),             title = "geom_density_2d_filled") +    geom_density_2d_filled(show.legend = FALSE)  p3 <- faithfuld |>    gg_blank(x = waiting,             y = eruptions,             x_breaks = scales::breaks_pretty(3),             title = "geom_contour") +    geom_contour(aes(z = density, col = after_stat(level)),                show.legend = FALSE)   p4 <- faithfuld |>    gg_blank(x = waiting,             y = eruptions,             x_breaks = scales::breaks_pretty(3),            title = "geom_contour_filled") +    geom_contour_filled(aes(z = density),                        show.legend = FALSE)  (p1 + p2) / (p3 + p4)

what: 5. Modifying a part of the colouring
title: Extending further
text: gg_* function contains geom can colour outlines fill, users can turn part using alpha = 0 pal = scales::alpha("red", 0). col variable specified, can use col = NA, fill = NA, col = "red" fill = "red" modify part colouring. , col = NULL. case, must use gg_blank.
code: p1 <- iris |>   gg_density(     x = Sepal.Length,     col = Species,      alpha = 0,      col_legend_nrow = 2,      col_labels = str_to_sentence)  p2 <- iris |>   gg_density(     x = Sepal.Length,     alpha = 0)  p3 <- iris |>   gg_density(     x = Sepal.Length,      col = Species,     pal = scales::alpha(pal_hue, 0),      col_legend_nrow = 2,      col_labels = str_to_sentence)  p4 <- iris |>   gg_density(     x = Sepal.Length,     pal = scales::alpha(pal_blue, 0))  (p1 + p2) / (p3 + p4)

title: Extending further
text: can create powerful custom functions. ... argument can allow access arguments within ggblanket gg_* function (applicable ggplot2::geom_* function).
code: gg_point_custom <- function(data, x, y, col,                              size = 3,                              shape = 17,                             pal = RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(8, "Dark2"),                              col_title = "",                              col_legend_place = "t",                             ...) {   data |>      gg_point(x = {{ x }}, y = {{ y }}, col = {{col}},               size = size,               shape = shape,              pal = pal,               col_title = col_title,               col_legend_place = col_legend_place,               col_labels = str_to_sentence,              ...) }  iris |>   gg_point_custom(     x = Sepal.Width,     y = Sepal.Length,     col = Species)

title: Extending further
text: ggblanket wraps ggplot2::geom_sf ggplot2::geom_raster functions, spatial vector array data can visualised.
code: sf::st_read(system.file("shape/nc.shp", package = "sf"),                    quiet = TRUE) |>   gg_sf(col = AREA,         pal = RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(9, "Blues")) +   ggeasy::easy_remove_axes() stars::read_stars(system.file("tif/L7_ETMs.tif", package = "stars")) |>  tibble::as_tibble() |>  gg_raster(x = x,            y = y,            col = L7_ETMs.tif,            facet = band,            col_title = "") +   ggeasy::easy_remove_axes()

title: Extending further
text: showtext sysfonts packages support use different fonts Google. theme_*_mode functions provide base_family, title_family, subtitle_family caption_family arguments. Fonts can identified Google fonts website.
code: sysfonts::font_add_google("Covered By Your Grace", "grace") sysfonts::font_add_google('Anton', 'anton') sysfonts::font_add_google('Syne Mono', 'syne')  showtext::showtext_auto(enable = TRUE)  penguins |>   gg_point(     x = flipper_length_mm,     y = body_mass_g,     col = sex,     facet = species,     title = "Penguins body mass by flipper length",     subtitle = "Palmer Archipelago, Antarctica",     caption = "Source: Gorman, 2020",     pal = c("#2596be", "#fc7c24"),     theme = light_mode(       base_family = "grace",        title_family = "anton",       subtitle_family = "syne")) showtext::showtext_auto(enable = FALSE)

title: Extending further
text: patchwork package enables plots patched together.
code: p1 <- mtcars |>    gg_point(x = mpg,             y = disp)  p2 <- mtcars |>    gg_boxplot(x = gear,               y = disp,               group = gear,               width = 0.5)  p3 <- mtcars |>    gg_smooth(x = disp,              y = qsec,              x_breaks = scales::breaks_pretty(4))  (p1 | p2) / p3

title: Extending further
text: ggtext package enables plot text use markdown syntax. applicable markdown theme elements need added plot. Note want title bold, ’s important change default theme plain title - otherwise gg_theme defaults bold.
code: penguins |> gg_blank( x = flipper_length_mm, y = body_mass_g, title = "**Bold** or _italics_ or red", theme = light_mode(title_face = "plain") ) + theme(plot.title = ggtext::element_markdown()) penguins |> gg_point( x = bill_length_mm, y = bill_depth_mm, col = species, facet = island, facet2 = sex, x_title = "**Bill length** (mm)", y_title = "**Bill depth** (mm)", col_title = "**Species**", title = "***Pygoscelis*** **penguin** bill lengths and depths", subtitle = "**Adelie**, **Chinstrap**, *and* **Gentoo**", x_labels = \(x) glue::glue("_{x}_"), y_labels = \(x) glue::glue("_{x}_"), col_labels = \(x) glue::glue("_{x}_"), facet_labels = \(x) glue::glue("_{x}_"), theme = light_mode(title_face = "plain") ) + theme( plot.title = ggtext::element_markdown(), plot.subtitle = ggtext::element_markdown(), axis.title.x = ggtext::element_markdown(), axis.title.y = ggtext::element_markdown(), axis.text.x = ggtext::element_markdown(), axis.text.y = ggtext::element_markdown(), legend.title = ggtext::element_markdown(), legend.text = ggtext::element_markdown(), strip.text.x = ggtext::element_markdown(), strip.text.y = ggtext::element_markdown())

title: Extending further
text: gghighlight package enables geoms parts thereof highlighted. noted gg_* function builds scale data thinks within panel. Therefore situations, need add *_limits = c(NA, NA) argument make sure scale builds include full height non-highlighted data.
code: penguins |>   gg_point(x = flipper_length_mm,            y = body_mass_g) +   gghighlight::gghighlight(body_mass_g >= 5000) iris |>   gg_histogram(     x = Sepal.Length,     col = Species,     facet = Species,     facet_labels = str_to_sentence,     pal = rep(pal_blue, 3),     y_limits = c(NA, NA)   ) +   gghighlight::gghighlight()

title: Extending further
text: ggdist package enables visualisation uncertainty. key making work ggblanket ensuring gg_blank builds distribution scale correctly. can hack adding *min *max aesthetics low high quantile distributions. However, might easier just use ggplot2 .
code: library(distributional)  d <- data.frame(   name = c("Gamma(2,1)", "Normal(5,1)", "Mixture"),   dist = c(     dist_gamma(2, 1),     dist_normal(5, 1),     dist_mixture(       dist_gamma(2, 1),        dist_normal(5, 1),        weights = c(0.4, 0.6))   ))   d |>   mutate(dist_min = quantile(dist, 0.001),           dist_max = quantile(dist, 0.999)) |> #to get scales functionally   gg_blank(     y = name,      xmin = dist_min, xmax = dist_max, #to get scales functionally     y_expand = c(0.05, 0.05),     y_title = "") +   ggdist::stat_slabinterval(     aes(dist = dist),     col = pal_blue,     fill = scales::alpha(pal_blue, 0.5)) d <- tibble(x = 1:10,        sd = seq(1, 3, length.out = 10)) |>   mutate(dist = distributional::dist_normal(x, sd))   d |>   mutate(dist_min = quantile(dist, 0.001),           dist_max = quantile(dist, 0.999)) |> #to get scales functionally   gg_blank(     x = x,     ymin = dist_min, ymax = dist_max) + #to get scales functionally   ggdist::stat_lineribbon(aes(ydist = dist)) +   scale_fill_brewer() +   labs(fill = "Level")

title: Extending further
text: plotly::ggplotly function enables interactive plots.
code: p <- diamonds |>   gg_bar(     x = color,     col = color,     width = 0.75,     theme = light_mode(base_family = "helvetica"))   plotly::ggplotly(p,    tooltip = c("x", "y"),    width = 500,    height = 300)

previous_headings: Overview > 9. Work with some extension packages
title: Extending further
text: ggiraph package enables interactive plots.
code: p <- diamonds |>   gg_blank(     x = color,     col = color,     width = 0.75,     y_include = 0,     stat = "count",      theme = light_mode(base_family = "helvetica", base_size = 9)) +   ggiraph::geom_bar_interactive(     aes(tooltip = after_stat(count),          data_id = color), width = 0.75)  ggiraph::girafe(   ggobj = p,    height_svg = 3,    width_svg = 5,   options = list(     ggiraph::opts_sizing(rescale = TRUE, width = 0.7),     ggiraph::opts_tooltip(use_fill = TRUE),      ggiraph::opts_hover(css = "stroke:black;stroke-width:1px;")))

title: Extending further
text: gganimate package enables animated plots.
code: gapminder::gapminder |>    gg_blank(     x = gdpPercap,      y = lifeExp,      col = country,      facet = continent,      x_trans = "log10",      pal = gapminder::country_colors,     col_legend_place = "n",      title = "Year: {frame_time}",      x_title = "GDP per capita",      y_title = "Life expectancy",      y_include = 0) +   geom_point(aes(size = pop), alpha = 0.7) +   scale_size(range = c(2, 12)) +   gganimate::transition_time(year) +   gganimate::ease_aes("linear")

title: ggblanket
text: simplify ggplot2 visualisation, ggblanket provides: thirty gg_* wrapper functions single col argument colour fill variable pal argument customise colours facet argument facet variable additional facet2 argument facet 2nd variable Prefixed arguments customise titles, scales, guides, faceting Unspecified x/y/col titles converted sentence case Ability set theme, including provided themes Pretty defaults symmetry Access geom_* arguments via … Ability add multiple geom_* layers framework able extended
code: library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) library(ggblanket) library(patchwork)  penguins <- palmerpenguins::penguins |>   mutate(sex = stringr::str_to_sentence(sex)) |>   tidyr::drop_na(sex)

title: ggblanket
text: gg_* function wraps ggplot2 ggplot(aes(...)) function applicable ggplot2 geom_*() function. aesthetics placed directly gg_* function: within ggplot2::aes function. also provides access arguments relevant stat function.
code: # ggplot2 penguins |>    ggplot() +    geom_point(aes(x = flipper_length_mm,                   y = body_mass_g)) # ggblanket penguins |>   gg_point(     x = flipper_length_mm,     y = body_mass_g)

what: 2. A single col argument to colour and fill by a variable
title: ggblanket
text: colour fill aesthetics ggplot2 merged single concept represented col argument. argument means everything coloured according , .e. points, lines polygon interiors.
code: # ggplot2 p1 <- penguins |>    ggplot() +    geom_point(aes(x = flipper_length_mm,                   y = body_mass_g,                  col = species)) +    theme(legend.position = "bottom") +   guides(col = guide_legend(title.position = "top")) +   labs(col = "Species") +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = scales::breaks_pretty(n = 3))  p2 <- penguins |>   ggplot() +   geom_density(aes(x = body_mass_g,                     fill = species)) +   theme(legend.position = "bottom") +   guides(fill = guide_legend(title.position = "top")) +   labs(fill = "Species")  p1 + p2 # ggblanket p1 <- penguins |>   gg_point(     x = flipper_length_mm,     y = body_mass_g,      col = species,      x_breaks = scales::breaks_pretty(n = 3),      col_legend_ncol = 2)  p2 <- penguins |>   gg_density(     x = body_mass_g,      col = species,     x_breaks = scales::breaks_pretty(n = 3),      col_legend_ncol = 2)  p1 + p2

what: 3. A pal argument to customise colours
title: ggblanket
text: pal argument used customise colours geom. user can provide vector colours argument. can named . works consistent way - regardless whether col argument added . named palette can used make individual colours stick particular values.
code: # ggplot2 p1 <- penguins |>   ggplot() +   geom_histogram(aes(x = body_mass_g),                  fill = "#1B9E77") +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = scales::breaks_pretty(n = 3))   p2 <- penguins |>   ggplot() +   geom_jitter(aes(x = species,                    y = body_mass_g,                    col = sex)) +   scale_color_manual(values = c("#2596be", "#fc7c24"))  p1 + p2 # ggblanket p1 <- penguins |>   gg_histogram(     x = body_mass_g,      pal = "#1B9E77",      x_breaks = scales::breaks_pretty(n = 3))  p2 <- penguins |>   gg_jitter(     x = species,      y = body_mass_g,      col = sex,      pal = c("#2596be", "#fc7c24"))  p1 + p2

what: 4. A facet argument to facet by a variable
title: ggblanket
text: Faceting treated aesthetic. Users just provide unquoted variable facet . single facet (facet2) variable provided, ’ll default “wrap” layout. users can change facet_layout = "grid" argument.
code: # ggplot2 penguins |>   ggplot() +   geom_violin(aes(x = sex,                    y = body_mass_g)) +   facet_wrap(~species) # ggblanket penguins |>   gg_violin(     x = sex,     y = body_mass_g,     facet = species)

what: 5. An additional facet2 argument to facet by a 2nd variable
title: ggblanket
text: facet2 argument also provided extra functionality flexibility. facetand facet2 variables provided, ’ll default “grid” layout facet facet2. users can change facet_layout = "wrap" argument.
code: # ggplot2 penguins |>   ggplot() +   geom_histogram(aes(x = flipper_length_mm)) +   facet_grid(species ~ sex) # ggblanket penguins |>   gg_histogram(     x = flipper_length_mm,     facet = sex,     facet2 = species)

what: 6. Prefixed arguments to customise titles, scales, guides, and faceting
title: ggblanket
text: Prefixed arguments available customise titles, scales, guides, faceting. designed work Rstudio auto-complete help users remember find adjustment need. Users first determine whether want change something relates x, y, col facet. type prefix press tab key access list options Rstudio auto-complete. can use arrow keys, press tab select want.
code: # ggplot2 penguins |>   ggplot() +   geom_jitter(aes(x = species,                    y = body_mass_g,                    col = sex)) +   expand_limits(y = 0) +   scale_x_discrete(labels = \(x) stringr::str_sub(x, 1, 1)) +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = scales::breaks_width(1500),                      labels = scales::label_number(big.mark = " "),                      trans = "sqrt") +   labs(x = "Species", y = "Body mass (g)", col = "Sex") +   theme(legend.position = "top") +   theme(legend.justification = "left") +   scale_colour_manual(values = scales::hue_pal()(2),                        guide = ggplot2::guide_legend(title.position = "top")) # ggblanket penguins |>   gg_jitter(     x = species,     y = body_mass_g,     col = sex,     x_labels = \(x) stringr::str_sub(x, 1, 1),     y_include = 0,     y_breaks = scales::breaks_width(1500),      y_labels = scales::label_number(big.mark = " "),      y_trans = "sqrt",     y_title = "Body mass (g)",      col_legend_place = "t")

what: 7. Unspecified x/y/col titles converted to sentence case
title: ggblanket
text: Unspecified x/y/col titles converted sentence case snakecase::to_sentence. titles can manually changed using *_title arguments. default conversion intended make titles sometimes able left .
code: # ggplot2 penguins |>   ggplot() +   geom_point(aes(x = flipper_length_mm,                   y = body_mass_g,                   col = sex)) +   facet_wrap(~species) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = scales::breaks_pretty(n = 3)) # ggblanket penguins |>   gg_point(       x = flipper_length_mm,       y = body_mass_g,        col = sex,       facet = species)

what: 8. Ability to set the theme, including to provided themes
title: ggblanket
text: ggplot2::theme_set function can used set theme globally, subsequent plots use theme argument plot left NULL. ggblanket provides two complete ggplot2 theme functions called light_mode (default) dark_mode. first argument base_size, changes size text title 10% higher caption 10% lower. arguments change family, face, colour, size, margins vertical adjustment text. Note gg_* function control gridlines present placement legend - despite theme provided. providing theme light_mode dark_mode, works best plot major vertical horizontal gridlines. users want everything adjusted per theme, can + theme onto plot instead. Note theme_set(theme_grey()) resets set theme ggplot2 code theme_grey ggblanket gg_* functions light_mode(). want ggblanket gg_* functions default using theme_grey(), must modify base_size slightly (e.g. theme_set(theme_grey(11.01))).
code: # ggblanket theme_set(dark_mode())  penguins |>   gg_point(     x = flipper_length_mm,     y = body_mass_g,     col = sex,     title = "Penguins body mass by flipper length",     subtitle = "Palmer Archipelago, Antarctica",     caption = "Source: Gorman, 2020",     pal = c("#2596be", "#fc7c24")) # ggblanket theme_set(light_mode(base_size = 12))  penguins |>   gg_point(     x = flipper_length_mm,     y = body_mass_g,     col = sex,     title = "Penguins body mass by flipper length",     subtitle = "Palmer Archipelago, Antarctica",     caption = "Source: Gorman, 2020",     pal = c("#2596be", "#fc7c24")) theme_set(theme_grey()) #unset the theme

title: ggblanket
text: orientation normal (.e. vertical): Default x scale limits expanding per ggplot2 defaults Default numeric y scale limits (non-transformed) start end break zero expanding Default categorical (numeric transformed) y scale limits expanding per ggplot2 defaults Default removal vertical gridlines opposite orientation horizontal (also keeps y categorical values correct order). Note gg_bin2d, gg_hex gg_raster deviate approach necessity, instead just place limits min max data default.
code: # ggplot2 penguins |>   group_by(species, sex) |>    summarise(body_mass_g = mean(body_mass_g)) |>    ggplot() +   geom_col(aes(x = body_mass_g,                 y = species,                 fill = sex),            position = "dodge",            width = 0.75) # ggblanket penguins |>   group_by(species, sex) |>    summarise(body_mass_g = mean(body_mass_g)) |>    gg_col(     x = body_mass_g,     y = species,     col = sex,     position = "dodge",     width = 0.75)

what: 10. Access to other geom_* arguments via ...
title: ggblanket
text: ... argument placed gg_* function within wrapped ggplot2::geom_* function. means arguments geom_* function available users (except mapping argument). Common arguments ... add size, linewidth width.
code: # ggblanket penguins |>   gg_smooth(     x = flipper_length_mm,     y = body_mass_g,     col = sex,     linewidth = 0.5, #accessed via geom_smooth     level = 0.99) #accessed via geom_smooth

what: 11. Ability to add multiple geom_* layers
title: ggblanket
text: Users can make plots multiple layers ggblanket adding ggplot2::geom_* layers. gg_* function puts aesthetics within wrapped ggplot function. Therefore, aesthetics inherit subsequent geoms added. Geoms plot order. gg_* function plot associated geom first layer, geoms plotted top .  geoms col aesthetic , recommended col aesthetic gg_* function. enables ggblanket legend placement, well ability adjust col scale via gg_* function. want col aesthetic bottom plot layer, require subsequent layers - use gg_blank function. Subsequent geom_* layers col aesthetic required can col = NA /fill = NA arguments - use inherit.aes = FALSE argument. Note plot scales constructed without knowledge subsequent layers. gg_* function builds x y scales based data, stat, x, y gg_* function. scales can adjusted accommodate subsequent layers using *_include *_limits arguments.
code: # ggblanket + ggplot2 penguins |>   gg_boxplot(x = species,              y = body_mass_g,              width = 0.5,              outlier.colour = NA) +   geom_jitter(col = pal_blue) # ggblanket + ggplot2 d <- penguins |>   group_by(species) |>   summarise(body_mass_g = mean(body_mass_g)) |>   mutate(lower = body_mass_g * 0.95) |>    mutate(upper = body_mass_g * 1.2)  p1 <- d |>   gg_blank(         y = species,     x = body_mass_g,     col = species,     xmin = lower,     xmax = upper,     x_include = 0,      x_labels = \(x) x / 1000,      x_title = "Body mass kg") +   geom_col(alpha = 0.9, width = 0.75) +   geom_errorbar(col = "black", width = 0.1)  p2 <- d |>   gg_blank(     y = species,     x = body_mass_g,     xmin = lower,      xmax = upper,      col = species,     x_include = 0,      x_labels = \(x) x / 1000,      x_title = "Body mass kg") +   geom_col(col = NA, fill = "#d3d3d3", width = 0.75) +   geom_errorbar(width = 0.1)  p1 / p2 # ggblanket + ggplot2 d_wide <- gapminder::gapminder |>   filter(year %in% c(1967, 2007)) |>   select(country, year, lifeExp) |>   tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = year, values_from = lifeExp) |>   mutate(gap = `2007` - `1967`) |>   slice_max(gap, n = 10) |>   mutate(country = forcats::fct_inorder(forcats::fct_drop(country)))   d_long <- d_wide |>   select(-gap) |>    tidyr::pivot_longer(-country,                        names_to = "year",                        values_to = "life_expectancy")  d_long |>    gg_blank(x = life_expectancy,            y = country,            col = year,            pal = pal_hue[c(2, 1)],            x_include = 0,            col_legend_place = "r",            title = "We're living longer",            subtitle = "Biggest life expectancy rise, 1967\u20132007",            x_title = "Life expectancy",             y_title = "") +   geom_segment(aes(x = `1967`, xend = `2007`,                     y = country, yend = country),                 data = d_wide, inherit.aes = FALSE,                 col = "#dddddd", linewidth = 2) +   geom_point(size = 2)

title: ggblanket 3.0.0
text: New theme functions: light_mode dark_mode. Breaking: removed gg_theme. Breaking: renamed *_grid arguments *_gridlines. Breaking: removed void argument. Breaking: removed gg_function. Breaking: made col_rescale require function (e.g. scales::rescale()). Added facet_switch argument. Made col_legend_place = "none" remove col legend. raster, removed col aesthetic legend work. Improved gg_sf default alpha. Supported gg_sf work non-standard named geometry.

title: ggblanket 2.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-05-26 Made ggblanket work better adding layers. Made pal longer inherit layers col aesthetic. Made x y scales work better, including NA limits. Added 2 colours pal_hue. Breaking: changed default *oob agument scales::oob_censor. Breaking: changed default coord agument include clip = "" argument. Breaking: removed add_tooltip function. Breaking: changed pal functions pal_blue, pal_grey, pal_hue.

title: ggblanket 1.8.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-04-24 Updated default colour palette. Updated default NA colour.

title: ggblanket 1.7.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-03-06 Updated gg_theme default axis-line gridline thickness. Supported named pal vectors. Removed clip argument. Added coord argument. Added *_oob arguments. Improved *_limits works. Removed default gridlines gg_sf. Fixed bug default x_expand. Fixed bug gg_histogram scales. Critical fix support dplyr 1.1.0. Supported colouring date, datetime time variables. Made datetime breaks default waiver. Breaking: removed void argument gg_theme. Corrected bug gg_raster legend showing. Updated gg_raster *_limits *_expand defaults.

title: ggblanket 1.6.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-11-18 Breaking: renamed add_tooltip_text add_tooltip changed column name “tooltip”. Breaking: coord argument removed. Added clip argument defaulted “”. Made x y limits also act within coord. Made gg_blank powerful flexible. Improved horizontal flipped plot scales. Fixed bugs relating free facet_scales scale limits. Improved vignette.

title: ggblanket 1.5.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-10-14 Breaking: redesigned gg_theme. Added automatic gridline removal. Added gg_bin2d function. Added gg_hex function. Added col_trans col_rescale arguments. Made col_legend_place default “r” numeric col variable. Made *_title = "" equivalent + labs(* = NULL). Supported x y datetime variables. Supported x y time variables. Redesigned internal code x, y col scales.

title: ggblanket 1.4.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-09-02 Breaking: Removed col_intervals argument. Breaking: Removed *_oob argument. Breaking: gg_theme, changed *_style arguments *_face. Breaking: gg_theme, changed font arguments family. Added gg_polygon function. Added col_continuous argument support colouring “steps”. Added col_legend_rev argument reverse legends. Added facet_layout argument faceting flexibility. Added facet_space argument support proportional facet panels. Removed width default. Improved default legend look continuous gradient. Improved scales y NULL plot horizontal. Fixed bug position = "fill". Updated messages. Added vignette.

title: ggblanket 1.3.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-08-05 Breaking: removed facet_intervals argument. Breaking: made extensive changes gg_theme function. Added facet2 aesthetic support effortless grid facetting. Changed default background theme colours. Fixed bug *_limits *_include working correctly. Added *_sec_axis arguments. Changed *_oob default scales::oob_keep. Removed reversal logical variable order. Defaulted col_legend_place "b".

title: ggblanket 1.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-07-01 Breaking: Removed *_breaks_n *_breaks_width. Added support *_breaks receive function. Added *_include argument. Added *_trans argument. Modified default x_breaks behaviour x y numeric date. Enhanced gg_blank work one x y. Provided support ggplotly. Added new add_tooltip_text function. Added new titles argument apply function across titles. Support gg_ functions working stats. Removed size argument, anticipation ggplot2 3.4.0. Updated gg_theme title vjust margins.

title: ggblanket 1.1.0
